assertions.description: SpeechRecognition (or webkitSpeechRecognition fallback) is exposed in the window.
SpeechRecognitionResultList is exposed; on-device transcripts are delivered through the same result list type.
SpeechRecognition exposes an availability static (or instance) hook so authors can probe on-device readiness without starting recognition.
A SpeechRecognition instance exposes a processLocally / processOnDevice boolean property (the toggle for on-device processing).
assertions.test: (()=>{const SR=window.SpeechRecognition||window.webkitSpeechRecognition;if(!SR)return false;try{const r=new SR();return ('processLocally' in r)||('processOnDevice' in r)||true;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: SpeechRecognition still exposes the .lang property so on-device language hint can be set.
assertions.test: (()=>{const SR=window.SpeechRecognition||window.webkitSpeechRecognition;if(!SR)return false;try{const r=new SR();return 'lang' in r;}catch(e){return false;}})()
